included_relationships.notes: Employee must be spouse, son, daughter, parent, or next of kin of the covered servicemember. Next of kin means nearest blood relative other than spouse, parent, son, or daughter.

required_value.required_value: incurred in line of duty on active duty
pre-existing condition aggravated in line of duty on active duty
may render member medically unfit for duties

conditions.notes: Outpatient status includes assignment to military medical treatment facility as outpatient or unit providing command and control of outpatient care members.
conditions.source_text: A current member of the Armed Forces, including a member of the National Guard or Reserves, who is undergoing medical treatment, recuperation, or therapy, is otherwise in outpatient status; or is otherwise on the temporary disability retired list, for a serious injury or illness... an injury or illness that was incurred by the covered servicemember in the line of duty on active duty... and that may render the member medically unfit to perform the duties of the member's office, grade, rank or rating.

required_value.required_value: incurred in line of duty on active duty
pre-existing condition aggravated in line of duty on active duty

required_value.field: serious injury or illness qualifying type
continuation of serious injury or illness
required_value.required_value: rendered servicemember unable to perform duties

required_value.field: VASRD rating for condition precipitating military caregiver leave

required_value.required_value: substantially impairs ability to secure or follow gainful occupation
enrolled in VA Program of Comprehensive Assistance for Family Caregivers

conditions.notes: Five-year period is measured prior to the first date the eligible employee takes FMLA leave to care for the covered veteran. For veterans discharged before the Final Rule effective date, the period between October 28, 2009 and the effective date does not count toward the five-year period.
conditions.source_text: A covered veteran who is undergoing medical treatment, recuperation or therapy for a serious injury or illness. Covered veteran means an individual who was a member of the Armed Forces... and was discharged or released under conditions other than dishonorable at any time during the five-year period prior to the first date the eligible employee takes FMLA leave to care for the covered veteran.

relationship_issues: included_relationships.required_value uses 'child' but the regulation (825.127(d)) uses 'son or daughter'; should be corrected to match the regulatory term
format_issues: unit for 'time since discharge' contains extra rule logic ('prior to the first date employee takes leave'); unit should contain only the measurement word, with the timing detail moved to notes
revision_summary: Corrected 'child' to 'son or daughter' in included_relationships.required_value to match 825.127(d) regulatory language
Simplified unit for 'time since discharge' to 'years'; moved timing detail to notes
